data.narrative:
Evozyne, a Series B biotech company founded by Andrew Ferguson, Rama Ranganathan, and Jeff Aronin, has raised $144M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 8 private peers and 3 public companies in the Biotech & Health sector, including Colossal Biosciences, Chai Discovery, New Limit, and Eikon Therapeutics at various stages.

Two valuation methods prove applicable. Stage-progression analysis, using the $144M raised as a proxy for current value and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical for Series B advancement, yields $274M. Peer-median triangulation across the four same-sector comparables—which span $539M (Eikon) to $10.3B (Colossal), with a median of $3.5B—directly implies $3.5B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied absent disclosed revenue figures. These two methods produce a triangulated range of $233M (low) to $4.0B (high), with a median of $1.9B across the applicable approaches.

The wide dispersion between stage-progression and peer-median results reflects material uncertainty around Evozyne's competitive position and capital efficiency relative to the cohort. Stage-progression assumes baseline growth; peer-median is pulled upward by high-valuation outliers like Colossal. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation, clinical or commercial traction relative to named peers, and capital requirements to next major milestone. The $1.9B midpoint represents a reasonable central estimate pending additional operational data.
